hero.subtitle: United States Marshals Service is the premier federal law enforcement unit in the San Andreas region. We serve justice and order.

mission.label: Our Mission
mission.title: Federal Law Enforcement
mission.p1: The United States Marshals Service is responsible for protecting the federal judiciary, apprehending fugitives, and operating witness protection programs in the San Andreas region.
mission.p2: As a unit, we operate with the highest standards of professionalism, justice, and public safety. Every operation is the product of rigorous planning and training.
mission.p3: Joining our ranks is not just a title—it is a responsibility and an honor.

footer.desc: District of San Andreas. Federal law enforcement unit serving under the principles of Justice, Integrity, and Service.
footer.copyright: © 2026 U.S. Marshals Service — District of San Andreas | FiveM Roleplay

about.label: About
about.title: HISTORY & MISSION
about.p1: The U.S. Marshals Service, established in 1789, is the oldest federal law enforcement agency in the United States. As the San Andreas District Office, our primary mission is to ensure the security of the federal judiciary, apprehend fugitives, and operate witness protection programs.
about.p2: Our unit operates under the principles of professionalism, discipline, and justice. Every agent undergoes rigorous training and commits to serving at the highest standards.
about.p3: Operating under the Department of Justice, our unit is a pillar of federal law enforcement.

justice.title: Justice
justice.text: Every individual's rights must be protected and equality before the law ensured.

integrity.title: Integrity
integrity.text: We carry out our duties with the highest ethical standards and transparency.

service.title: Service
service.text: Serving society and justice is our highest duty.

chiefMarshal.desc: The highest-ranking authority. Bears ultimate responsibility for all operations and personnel.

supervisoryDeputy.desc: Responsible for managing operational units and coordinating training programs.

seniorDeputy.desc: Senior field agent. Leads complex operations and trains new agents.

deputyMarshal.desc: Field agent. Carries out arrests, courthouse security, and witness protection duties.

rules.label: Regulations
rules.title: UNIT RULES
rules.intro: The following rules are binding for all U.S. Marshals Service personnel. Violations will result in disciplinary action.
rules.note: These rules are applied in addition to general server rules. All personnel are also required to comply with server rules.

items.1.1: All agents shall conduct themselves professionally while on duty.
items.1.2: Unit internal communication shall be conducted only through designated channels.
items.1.3: Rank hierarchy shall be followed under all circumstances.
items.1.4: Approval from higher authority shall be obtained for operations outside the unit.
items.1.5: Sharing classified information is strictly prohibited.
items.2.1: Standard unit uniform shall be worn during duty.
items.2.2: Badge and identification shall always be visibly displayed.
items.2.3: Tactical equipment shall only be used in approved operations.
items.2.4: Vehicle use shall be for official purposes only.
items.3.1: A briefing shall be conducted before each operation and task assignment shall be determined.
items.3.2: Use of force shall only be authorized in cases of self-defense or imminent threat to life.
items.3.3: Arrest procedures shall be conducted in accordance with standard operating procedures.
items.3.4: Post-operation reports shall be completed within 24 hours.
items.4.1: Official codes and terminology shall be used in radio communications.
items.4.2: Designated emergency call procedures shall be followed in emergencies.
items.4.3: Unit identity shall be clearly stated in external communications.

exp.q: How much experience is required to become a US Marshal?
exp.a: At least 2 weeks of active roleplay experience on the server is required. Experience on other servers is an advantage but not mandatory.

process.q: How long does the application process take?
process.a: Applications are typically reviewed within 48-72 hours. Suitable candidates are invited to the interview process.

training.q: How does the training process work?
training.a: Accepted candidates undergo a one-week basic training program covering operational procedures, communication protocols, and tactical training.

activity.q: Is there a minimum weekly activity requirement?
activity.a: Yes, all agents are expected to perform at least 10 hours of active duty per week. Leave forms must be submitted for extended absences.

transfer.q: Can I transfer from another unit?
transfer.a: Yes, transfers from other units are possible. Your current rank and experience are taken into consideration.

promotion.q: How does the internal promotion system work?
promotion.a: Promotions are evaluated by the chain of command based on performance, activity, and leadership qualities. Minimum wait times vary by rank.

apply.label: Registration
apply.title: APPLICATION FORM
apply.intro: Complete the form below to join the U.S. Marshals Service roster. Your application will be evaluated by our chain of command.
apply.prereq1: You must be an active member of our Discord server
apply.prereq2: You must be at least 18 years old
apply.prereq3: You must have at least 2 weeks of experience on the server
apply.prereq4: You must not have an active disciplinary record
